SOP-BESS-2569-12.title: BESS Dispatch and Pre-Charge Operations
SOP-BESS-2569-12.section_4.1: If mainland bottleneck is predicted and current SoC < 50%, initiate pre-charge sequence immediately using excess mainland power before the bottleneck window begins.
SOP-BESS-2569-12.section_4.2: During PV ramp-down events, BESS should be prioritized to cover the deficit before starting diesel generators to minimize fuel consumption.
SOP-BESS-2569-12.section_4.3: Avoid cycling BESS below 20% SoC unless in a critical N-1 contingency event.

SOP-DG-2569-04.title: Diesel Generator Commitment and Ramping
SOP-DG-2569-04.section_3.1: Minimum runtime for any diesel generator unit is 2 hours. Do not commit units for transient peaks < 30 minutes.
SOP-DG-2569-04.section_3.2: Warm-up sequence requires 15 minutes. If an event is predicted within 30 minutes and BESS cannot cover the load, idle warming must begin immediately.
SOP-DG-2569-04.section_3.3: Ramp rate must not exceed 2 MW per minute. For steep ramps, stage multiple units to spread the load.

SOP-GRID-2569-01.title: Mainland HVDC Cable Constraints
SOP-GRID-2569-01.section_2.1: Maximum continuous import limit is 16 MW. Temporary overload to 18 MW is permitted for a maximum of 15 minutes.
SOP-GRID-2569-01.section_2.2: If HVDC loading exceeds 85%, operator must prepare island generation (Diesel) to relieve cable stress.
SOP-GRID-2569-01.section_2.3: In case of N-1 cable failure, immediately shed non-critical loads and ramp all available diesel units to maximum capacity.
